meta.title: From 430 Lines to Data-Driven: Sidebar Component Refactor
meta.excerpt: How eliminating hardcoded markup reduced complexity and enabled configuration over customization

props.text: The Sidebar component was 430 lines of hardcoded conditional rendering. Nested ternaries, repeated markup patterns, state scattered across component levels. Adding a menu item meant touching 5 files. Reordering items required touching 3 locations. A simple feature took 4 hours to implement.

steps.title: Adding a Menu Item
steps.description: Required updating Sidebar, NavConfig, types.ts, tests, and Storybook. 4 files, 4 hours.

steps.title: Reordering Items
steps.description: Hardcoded order meant manual copy-paste reordering. Cascaded through multiple locations.

steps.title: Changing Icons
steps.description: Icon imports scattered through the component. Changing icon set required find-and-replace.

steps.title: Permission Control
steps.description: No clear pattern for role-based visibility. Conditions sprinkled throughout, hard to audit.

steps.title: Testing
steps.description: Testing required mocking 20+ nested components. Test file was longer than component.

stats.context: Mistakes in conditional logic, state sync

props.text: Instead of hardcoding markup, we externalized the menu structure into a configuration file. The component became a pure renderer of that structure. Changes to the menu meant updating data, not code.

props.title: Key Pattern: Separation of Concerns
props.body: Configuration defines WHAT to display (data). Component defines HOW to display it (rendering). This separation enables changes to one without touching the other. Want to reorder? Change data. Want different styling? Change component. Want different behaviors? Change logic. Each change is isolated.

stats.context: Data vs code changes are clear and isolated
Can use same config in multiple places
Can switch configs based on user role
Logic separated from data, easier to verify

steps.title: Identify Repetition Patterns
steps.description: When you copy-paste code 5+ times, extract to a loop with data.

steps.title: Externalize Decisions
steps.description: Hardcoded conditions often represent decisions that should be data.

steps.title: Make Components Data-Agnostic
steps.description: Components should not care where data comes from, only how to render it.

steps.title: Measure Before & After
steps.description: Track metrics: lines of code, time to feature, test coverage. Data guides decisions.

props.text: Refactoring from 430 hardcoded lines to a 65-line data-driven component wasn't just about code cleanup. It transformed how the team thinks about configuration. What used to take 4 hours now takes 2 minutes. Tests become simpler. Bugs become rarer. The pattern scales: use it for forms, tables, modals, anything with repeated structure. Data-driven design isn't advanced—it's the path to sustainable software.
